q: They (stand) at the gate all evening.
a: They were standing at the gate all evening.

q: The wind (blow) strongly throughout the night.
a: The wind was blowing strongly throughout the night.

q: We (cycle) to the farm at 7 o'clock yesterday morning.
a: We were cycling to the farm at 7 o'clock yesterday morning.

q: What (you both discuss) at 11 o'clock last night?
a: What were you both discussing at 11 o'clock last night?

q: The farmer (plough) his field all yesterday afternoon.
a: The farmer was ploughing his field all yesterday afternoon.
The women were quarrelling all this morning.
The birds were singing all morning.

q: I (walk) home at 9 o'clock last Tuesday morning.
a: I was walking home at 9 o'clock last Tuesday morning.

q: He (dig) in the garden all yesterday evening.
a: He was digging in the garden all yesterday evening.

q: We (have) dinner at 7 o'clock last night.
a: We were having dinner at 7 o'clock last night.

q: I (read) a novel at 8 o'clock last night.
a: I was reading a novel at 8 o'clock last night.

q: My uncle (fish) in the pond the whole afternoon.
a: My uncle was fishing in the pond the whole afternoon.

q: The boys (practise) for the sports all last week.
a: The boys were practising for the sports all last week.

q: Mother (make) my dress at 10 o'clock this morning.
a: Mother was making my dress at 10 o'clock this morning.

q: She (shop) in town all yesterday afternoon.
a: She was shopping in town all yesterday afternoon.
